title: Phase 4: JPEG2000 (5.40) + PNG (5.41)
description: Compressed grids. Pull in a Rust j2k/png decode path. DONE = agreement for 5.40/5.41.

title: P0.4: Differential harness skeleton
description: Implement the differential comparator in tests/: loads fixtures from manifest, stubs out the decode-and-compare loop, emits per-template coverage report. Passes cargo test with 0% agreement (framework is wired, decoder not yet implemented).

title: Phase 3: Complex packing (5.2/5.3) + spatial differencing
description: The trickiest unpacker; unlocks most HRRR fields. DONE = agreement coverage for 5.2/5.3 within tolerance; dashboard stays green.

title: P0.2: GRIB2 data model types
description: Define core types in gribtract-core: Field, GridValues, Level, ForecastTime, Ensemble, GridDefinition, ParameterId. These are the structs the comparator operates on. No I/O logic yet - just types with derives.

title: Phase 5: Grid geometry breadth
description: Lambert conformal (HRRR native), polar stereographic, Gaussian, rotated. DONE = geometry matches reference for these 3.x templates (corner points + scan mode).

title: Genesis: gribtract Implementation
description:
## Genesis Bead
Tied to plan: docs/plan/plan.md

## Overview
Pure-Rust GRIB2 decoder, verified field-by-field against eccodes/wgrib2 over a corpus of real NOAA products. Oracle-first: the differential harness gates every change. Two coupled north-stars (priority order): agreement coverage (gate) then decode throughput (proof, shown on the dashboard).

## Progress
- [ ] Phase 0: Oracle harness first
- [ ] Phase 1: Framing + metadata
- [ ] Phase 2: Simple packing (5.0) + lat/lon grid (3.0)
- [ ] Phase 2b: Proof-of-speed dashboard
- [ ] Phase 3: Complex packing (5.2/5.3) + spatial differencing
- [ ] Phase 4: JPEG2000 (5.40) + PNG (5.41)
- [ ] Phase 5: Grid geometry breadth
- [ ] Phase 6: Ensembles + statistical products (GEFS)
- [ ] Phase 7: Publish + integrate

comments.author: cli
comments.text: Phase 0 complete (2026-06-19). Differential harness is green: corpus loader, golden loader, field-by-field comparator with derived-tolerance policy, eccodes reference runner (scripts/gen_golden.py). Framework scores any decoder against committed goldens. Bead bf-qfj tracks generating real-data goldens in internal-cluster.

comments.author: cli
comments.text: Phase 1 complete (bead bf-9tc closed). Section 0-8 splitter + GDT 3.0 + PDT 4.0 + DRT 5.0 simple packing decoder implemented. Differential harness: 1/1 (100%) agreement on gfs_anl_t2m_5x5 fixture. Phase 2 (simple packing) is also satisfied for the existing corpus.

comments.author: cli
comments.text: Phase 2 complete (2026-06-19): GDT=0/PDT=0/DRT=0 (lat/lon grid + simple packing 5.0) — 1/1 fixtures matched (100% agreement). Agreement ratchet tightened to 100%. Bead bf-9s6 closed.

title: P2c speed: geometry + bitmap-rank cache reused across forecast hours
description: The grid geometry and bitmap are identical for every forecast hour in a cycle. Compute station->grid-index (and bitmap rank/select) ONCE per grid, reuse across all messages. Measure stations x hours/sec before/after; keep only if correct + faster, else revert and log in notes/parse-speed-log.md.

title: P2c speed: parallelism across forecast-hour messages
description: Fan the per-forecast-hour messages across cores to build the time series concurrently. Watch for false sharing / allocation contention. Keep only if faster + correct; revert + log otherwise.

title: P2c.0: Fixed-station point-extraction benchmark harness
description: Build the station point-extraction benchmark per docs/notes/station-extraction-benchmark.md: fixed roster of ~7 US metro coordinates, assemble per-station time series (one value per forecast hour across a cycle), nearest + bilinear modes. Verify every extracted value against the full-grid decode at the same point within derived tolerance. Emit bench-results.json records with workload='station-extract' (stations x hours/sec, agreement, git_sha, host) and add a dashboard view. Frame generically (point forecasts at fixed stations) — do not name any downstream consumer.

title: Generate real-data golden fixtures on the internal eccodes cluster
description: scripts/gen_golden.py is the eccodes reference runner but eccodes is not on this server. Run it on the internal cluster (see gitignored .marathon/local-env.md for the concrete cluster + archive names) to generate golden JSON for at least one real GFS/ECMWF GRIB2 file from the upstream archive. Steps: (1) copy scripts/gen_golden.py + a real .grib2 to a pod; (2) pip install eccodes; (3) python3 gen_golden.py <file> <fixture_id>; (4) commit tests/corpus/golden/<fixture_id>.json + update tests/corpus/manifest.json. Acceptance: at least one real golden in the corpus, harness still green.

title: P2c speed: SIMD unpack of bit-packed integer spans
description: Vectorize the bit-unpacking of the spans containing target points. Compare points/sec; revert + log if no gain or any correctness drift.

title: Phase 7: Publish + integrate
description: crates.io + Python bindings + the forecast-timeseries emitter consumed by the downstream backtest asof-join. DONE = published crate + integration path documented.

title: Phase 2b: Proof-of-speed dashboard
description: Benchmark harness emits bench-results.json + bench-history.jsonl (throughput + agreement vs eccodes/wgrib2). Self-contained offline HTML dashboard renders the 4 views; 'gribtract serve' live mode runs a real decode from the browser over SSE. Mount Agentation. Spec: docs/notes/throughput-dashboard.md. DONE = dashboard renders offline, live mode proves a real decode, every speed figure paired with agreement+provenance, CI publishes it as an artifact.

title: Phase 6: Ensembles + statistical products (GEFS)
description: Product templates 4.1/4.8/4.11 for members + time-aggregated fields. DONE = agreement for GEFS ensemble/statistical products.

title: Static HTML dashboard: 4 views from bench-results.json
description: Build a self-contained single HTML file (assets/dashboard.html or similar) that renders all 4 dashboard views from bench-results.json: (1) Headline big numbers (MB/s, messages/s, grid-points/s, speedup vs reference), (2) Throughput over commits time-series from bench-history.jsonl, (3) Grouped bars by 5.x template, (4) Agreement coverage companion. Zero external CDN dependencies — everything inline (plain canvas/SVG or vendored chart lib). Agentation mounted. DONE = open the HTML file offline, all 4 views render from the JSON files.

title: xtask bench: emit bench-results.json + bench-history.jsonl
description: Implement 'xtask bench' command: run gribtract decoder on the inline corpus, measure wall-clock throughput (MB/s, messages/s, grid-points/s), record agreement percentage from the differential harness, write bench-results.json with full provenance (git_sha, timestamp, host cpu/cores/mem, corpus stats), and append one entry to bench-history.jsonl. Schema matches docs/plan/plan.md 'Benchmark result'. No dashboard rendering yet — just the data pipeline. DONE = xtask bench runs, bench-results.json and bench-history.jsonl are written, cargo test still passes.

title: Phase 7: Publish + integrate
description: crates.io + Python bindings + the forecast-timeseries emitter consumed by a downstream backtest asof-join. DONE = published crate + integration path documented.

title: Phase 0: Oracle harness first
description: Stand up the corpus loader, eccodes/wgrib2 reference runner, field-by-field comparator, and derived-tolerance policy (docs/notes/oracle-and-tolerance.md). Scaffold the Cargo workspace (gribtract-core / gribtract / gribtract-cli / xtask). Wire 'cargo test' to run a sampled differential suite. DONE = a green framework that can score ANY decoder against the corpus, even though the decoder doesn't decode yet.

title: gribtract serve: live mode HTTP server with SSE
description: Implement 'gribtract serve --bench <corpus>' (or 'xtask serve'): starts a local HTTP server that serves the dashboard HTML + exposes POST /run endpoint. Clicking Run benchmark triggers a real decode of the corpus, streams progress over SSE (Content-Type: text/event-stream), updates headline numbers live with a wall-clock timer. The server writes a fresh bench-results.json on each run and pushes the data to the browser. DONE = browser can trigger a real decode, see live streaming progress, and see updated numbers without a page reload.

title: Phase 2: Simple packing (5.0) + lat/lon grid (3.0)
description: First end-to-end numeric agreement on common GFS/NBM fields. DONE = agreement coverage > 0 for template 5.0/3.0, within derived tolerance.

title: Phase 1: Framing + metadata
description: Section 0-8 split, multi-field messages, identification/grid/product metadata. DONE = all non-value metadata fields match the reference exactly for GFS surface temp, gated by the harness.

title: Generate real-data golden fixtures in internal-cluster
description: scripts/gen_golden.py is the eccodes reference runner but eccodes is not available on this server. Run in internal-cluster (ECMWF toolchain pod) to generate golden JSON files for at least one real GFS/ECMWF GRIB2 file (e.g. from the upstream GRIB2 archive). Steps: (1) copy scripts/gen_golden.py + a real .grib2 to a pod; (2) pip install eccodes; (3) python3 gen_golden.py <file> <fixture_id>; (4) commit the resulting tests/corpus/golden/<fixture_id>.json + update tests/corpus/manifest.json. Acceptance: at least one real golden in the corpus, harness still green.

title: P0.1: Cargo workspace scaffold + empty crate stubs
description: Create the top-level Cargo.toml workspace, empty crates (gribtract-core, gribtract, gribtract-cli, xtask), .gitignore for target/, and confirm cargo check passes with no code yet. Acceptance: cargo check --workspace succeeds.

title: P0.3: Fixture corpus manifest + loader
description: Create tests/corpus/ structure with a real small GRIB2 fixture (fetch from NOAA or from internal-cluster), an in-tree manifest.json with sha256 + provenance, and a Rust fixture loader that checks the sha256 and returns raw bytes.

title: P2c speed: lazy/partial unpack for simple packing (5.0)
description: For template 5.0 the value at point i is a pure function of the packed bits at offset i — decode only the cells covering target points instead of the full grid. Verify equality vs full-grid decode at the points. Expect this to NOT generalize to complex packing; record that. Revert + log if it doesn't help.
